task.targetDescription: Test the system in the embedded environment and document every defect you find.
task.targetErrorTitle: The test environment could not be loaded
task.targetErrorDescription: The page refused to be embedded or is not responding. Open it in a new tab.
task.targetMobileDescription: On small screens the test environment is not embedded. Open it in a new tab and switch back here to write your answer.
task.answerPlaceholder: Describe your approach, your test cases and your results…
task.answerHint: Write so that someone without context can follow your work.
task.hintsDescription: Every hint you reveal is logged for your trainer.
task.hintNone: There are no hints for this task.
task.defectDescription: Document the defect the way you would on a real project.
task.defectSummaryPlaceholder: Short and unambiguous, e.g. “Login accepts an empty password”
task.defectSummaryHint: One sentence that makes the defect recognisable.
task.defectUrlHint: The address in the test environment where the defect occurs.
task.defectStepsPlaceholder:
1. Open the home page
2. Submit the login form without a password
3. …
task.defectStepsHint: Numbered, one step per line. Someone else has to be able to reproduce the defect from it.
task.defectExpectedPlaceholder: What should have happened according to the requirement?
task.confirmDescription: Once submitted you can no longer change your answer. It goes to your trainer for review.
task.validationAnswer: Please describe your approach before you submit.
task.validationDefect: Please fill in the summary, the steps, and the expected and actual results.
task.submittedDescription: Your work is queued for review. You will be notified once it has been reviewed.
task.submittedReadOnly: This submission has been sent and can no longer be changed.
task.revisionDescription: Your trainer would like you to revise this task. Adjust your answer and submit it again.
task.askQuestion: Ask a question about this task
task.lockedDescription: You cannot work on it yet.
task.notStartedDescription: Your attempt starts as soon as you begin working. Your input is saved automatically.
task.errorEvidence: This task requires a defect report with an address. Please complete the defect report.
task.errorConflict: This task was changed in another tab. Please reload the page.
task.errorSubmit: Your work could not be submitted.

landing.title: You learn software testing by testing.
landing.subtitle: DiTeLe combines theory with real practice: you test running applications, write professional defect reports and get feedback from experienced trainers.
landing.valueSubtitle: Theory is everywhere. The difference is the practice behind it.
landing.value1Body: Every practical task opens a running web application right next to the brief. No screenshot, no video — you test it yourself.
landing.value2Title: Defect reports like on the job
landing.value2Body: Title, steps, expected and actual behaviour, severity: you learn the structure development teams actually read.
landing.value3Body: Every submission is reviewed and commented on. You see exactly what convinced and what is still missing.
landing.howSubtitle: Four steps that repeat for every task — until the technique sticks.
landing.step1Body: Short units of video, script and quiz cover the theory.
landing.step2Body: The practical task opens a real test environment in the browser.
landing.step3Body: You document your findings as a structured defect report.
landing.step4Body: Your trainer reviews the submission and replies.
landing.coursesSubtitle: Hands-on courses on test design, test execution and defect reporting.
landing.coursesEmptyBody: As soon as the first course is published, it will appear here.
landing.coursesErrorBody: The catalogue cannot be reached right now. Please try again later.
landing.finalBody: Create a free account, request a course and start with your first practical task.

about.title: About us
about.lead: DiTeLe is the WAMOCON Academy learning platform for hands-on software testing.
about.missionBody: You do not learn testing by memorising definitions. You learn it by having a real application in front of you, working through it systematically and writing down what you find. That is exactly what DiTeLe is built for: short theory, plenty of practice, and feedback from people who do the job.
about.approach1Body: Theory units are short and serve the next task. The focus is on the testing itself.
about.approach2Body: Every submission is a structured defect report. What you end up with is a record that shows what you can do.
about.approach3Body: Experienced testers review submissions, answer questions and give specific guidance.
about.audienceBody: For people starting out in testing, for career changers from adjacent IT roles, and for teams that want to bring their testing practice to a shared level. No programming experience is needed.
about.contactBody: Your contact person at WAMOCON Academy answers questions about the platform or about access.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.description: Answers to the questions asked most often before a first course.
faq.a1: DiTeLe is the WAMOCON Academy learning platform for hands-on software testing. It combines short theory units with practical tasks on real applications and with feedback from trainers.
faq.a2: No. The foundation courses do not assume any programming knowledge. Being comfortable with a web browser is enough to get started.
faq.a3: A course consists of several stages, and each stage of individual tasks. Theory tasks teach the material, practical tasks let you apply it straight away. You work at your own pace.
faq.a4: In a practical task, a running test application opens right next to the task brief. You test the application yourself and document your findings as a defect report with steps, expected behaviour and actual behaviour.
faq.a5: A WAMOCON Academy trainer. You either get an acceptance with a comment, or a request for revision with specific guidance.
faq.q6: How much time do I have?
faq.a6: Tasks can have a start date and a deadline, and you see both in the course overview. Without a deadline you work at your own pace.
faq.a7: Yes. If a revision is requested, you open the task again, add to your answer and submit it once more. Your draft is kept in the process.
faq.q8: Which languages is the platform available in?
faq.a8: The interface is available in German, English and Russian. Course content is available in the language it was published in.
faq.q9: How do I get access to a course?
faq.a9: Create an account, open the course in the catalogue and send a request. An administrator reviews the request and assigns you to a cohort.
faq.a10: Use the "Forgot your password?" link on the sign-in page. You will receive an email with a link that lets you set a new password.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.lead: How DiTeLe handles your personal data.
privacy.pendingNotice: The full privacy policy, including the details of the controller, will be added before launch. The overview below describes what data the platform actually processes.
privacy.s1Body: The controller for the processing is WAMOCON GmbH as the operator of the WAMOCON Academy. You will find the full contact details in the legal notice.
privacy.s2Body: Account data (name, email address, language, time zone), learning data (course requests, cohort membership, task work, submissions, trainer assessments, questions and answers) and technical logs required for operation and security.
privacy.s3Body: The processing serves the delivery of the learning programme and takes place in order to perform the usage relationship (Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR) and to protect legitimate interests in secure operation (Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR).
privacy.s4Body: Trainers see the submissions and questions of the cohorts they look after. Administrators see accounts, cohorts and course requests. Any access beyond that is technically ruled out by permission rules in the database.
privacy.s5Body: Account and learning data are stored for the duration of the learning relationship and then deleted or anonymised, unless a statutory retention obligation applies.
privacy.s6Body: DiTeLe uses strictly necessary cookies only. They keep you signed in and store your choice of light or dark appearance. There is no tracking and no advertising.
privacy.s7Body: You have the right to information, rectification, erasure, restriction of processing, data portability and objection, as well as the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority.
privacy.s8Body: For data protection matters, please contact your contact person at WAMOCON Academy or the address given in the legal notice.

legal.title: Legal notice
legal.lead: Information pursuant to section 5 DDG (German Digital Services Act).
legal.pendingNotice: The full mandatory details (address, authorised representatives, commercial register, VAT identification number) will be added before launch.
legal.s1Body: WAMOCON GmbH, operator of the WAMOCON Academy and of the DiTeLe learning platform.
legal.s2Body: Please address enquiries about the platform to your contact person at WAMOCON Academy.
legal.s3Body: Editorial responsibility for the course content lies with the WAMOCON Academy.
legal.s4Body: As a service provider we are responsible for our own content on these pages under the general laws. However, we are not obliged to monitor third-party information that has been transmitted or stored.
legal.s5Body: Our offering contains links to external third-party websites over whose content we have no influence. The respective provider is always responsible for the content of the linked pages.
legal.s6Body: The course content and works on these pages are subject to German copyright law. Reproduction or distribution outside the learning programme requires consent.
